blog_posts.title: Bridal Hair Trends 2025
blog_posts.summary: From soft braided crowns to pearl-studded low buns, explore the most requested bridal hair trends for 2025.
blog_posts.content:
2025 is all about soft texture, romantic details, and practical elegance. This year, we’re seeing a rise in effortless looks that still feel special on camera.

Key trends:

1. Romantic braided crowns
Our most-booked look is the **Romantic Braided Crown 2025**, a soft crown braid paired with loose face-framing pieces. It’s ideal for outdoor and destination weddings because it keeps hair off the neck while still looking whimsical.

2. Pearl-studded low buns
Sleek, low buns are still in—but 2025 versions include scattered pearls and delicate pins. The **Pearl-Studded Low Bun 2025** combines a clean silhouette with subtle sparkle that reads beautifully in photos.

3. Textured long updos with veils
Brides with long hair are embracing airy, textured updos that support longer veils without feeling heavy. Styles like our **Soft Romantic Updo with Cathedral Veil** and **Twisted Low Updo with Sheer Veil** are perfect examples.

4. Statement accessories
From sparkling clips to heirloom combs, accessories are a defining feature of 2025 looks. We recommend choosing one hero accessory and building the hairstyle around it.

Recommended looks:
- Romantic Braided Crown 2025
- Pearl-Studded Low Bun 2025
- Soft Romantic Updo with Cathedral Veil
- Textured Chignon Updo with Veil

Save your favorites from the gallery, then bring them to your trial so your stylist can tailor each trend to your hair type and dress neckline.

blog_posts.title: The Ultimate Bridal Hair Timeline: From Trial to Wedding Day
blog_posts.summary: Exactly when to book your trial, confirm your package, and share your inspiration photos.
blog_posts.content:
Planning your bridal hair doesn’t have to be stressful. Use this timeline to stay ahead:

6–9 months out:
- Research stylists and save inspiration to your favorites.
- Inquire about destination options if you’re getting married away from home.

3–4 months out:
- Book your **Bridal Hair Trial** on a weekday evening so you can come after work.
- Confirm your bridal package and approximate party size.

1–2 months out:
- Finalize your veil and accessories.
- Share any last-minute changes with your stylist.

Week of the wedding:
- Reconfirm timing and location.
- Deep condition your hair 3–4 days before the wedding, not the night before.

blog_posts.title: How to Choose a Veil-Friendly Bridal Hairstyle
blog_posts.summary: Tips for pairing your veil length with the perfect updo, half-up, or down style.
blog_posts.content: Your veil placement can completely change how your hairstyle reads from the front and back. For cathedral-length veils, we love structured updos like the **Soft Romantic Updo with Cathedral Veil** or **Classic Long Veil Updo**. Birdcage veils pair beautifully with vintage-inspired waves and short styles.

blog_posts.title: Real Wedding Spotlight: Romantic City Hall Elopement in San Francisco
blog_posts.summary: A chic low bun and simple veil for an intimate SF City Hall ceremony.
blog_posts.content: For this San Francisco City Hall elopement, our stylist Elena created a simple, polished low bun that worked perfectly with a fingertip veil and structured dress. The bride booked our **City Hall Bride-Only Quick Style** package and opted for in-salon styling before heading to the ceremony.

blog_posts.title: Destination Wedding Hair Tips for Beach and Tropical Ceremonies
blog_posts.summary: Humidity-proof styling strategies for Maui, Mexico, and beyond.
blog_posts.content: Destination weddings require extra planning for your hair. In humid locations like Maui, we recommend textured updos or braided styles that age well throughout the day. When you submit a quote request for Maui, be sure to include your ideal start time, getting-ready location, and a rough budget so we can suggest the best combination of bride and bridal party services.

blog_posts.title: Bridal Hair Trials: What to Expect and How to Prepare
blog_posts.summary: Everything you need to know before your bridal hair trial appointment.
blog_posts.content: We recommend scheduling your **Bridal Hair Trial** on a weekday to ensure your stylist has ample time to experiment. Evening appointments (5 pm and later) are ideal if you’re coming straight from work. Bring your veil, accessories, and 3–5 favorite saved styles from the gallery—especially any 2025 trend looks you’re considering.

blog_posts.title: Top 10 Bridesmaid Hairstyles That Photograph Beautifully
blog_posts.summary: Our favorite cohesive-yet-individual looks for bridesmaid parties of all sizes.
blog_posts.content: For bridesmaids, we love a mix of soft updos, low ponytails, and romantic half-up styles. Our **Bridesmaid Basic In-Salon Styling** option is perfect for parties who want beautiful, cohesive looks on a budget.

blog_posts.title: Humidity-Proof Summer Bridal Hairstyles
blog_posts.summary: Beat the heat with styles that last through outdoor ceremonies and late-night dancing.
blog_posts.content: Textured chignons, braided crowns, and polished low buns hold up best in humid summer weather. Consider adding a sparkling clip or comb to draw attention to the back of your dress.

faq_items.question: What is your cancellation policy?
faq_items.answer:
We understand that plans can change, and we aim to be as fair as possible while protecting reserved dates and stylist time.

• At the time of booking, a non-refundable retainer of 30% of the total services is required to secure your wedding date.

• Cancellations made more than 30 days before the wedding date: Your retainer remains non-refundable but may be converted into a studio credit that can be used toward future services (trials, special event styling, or a new wedding date) within 12 months, subject to availability. No additional cancellation fees are charged.

• Cancellations made 8–30 days before the wedding date: Your retainer is forfeited and an additional 25% of the remaining balance may be charged to compensate for the reserved date and lost bookings.

• Cancellations made 7 days or fewer before the wedding date or no-shows: The full remaining balance is due and may be charged to the card on file.

Destination weddings may have additional non-refundable travel or lodging fees. Please review your specific contract for full details.

faq_items.question: Can I reschedule my booking to a new date?
faq_items.answer:
Yes, one complimentary date change is typically allowed if requested at least 30 days before your original wedding date and if your stylist is available for the new date. Your retainer will be transferred to the new date.

If you request additional date changes or a date change within 30 days of the event, a rescheduling fee may apply and availability is not guaranteed. Destination weddings may incur additional change fees related to travel or lodging adjustments.

faq_items.question: Do you require a retainer or deposit to book?
faq_items.answer:
Yes. A signed contract and a non-refundable retainer of 30% of your total estimated services are required to reserve your wedding date. Your date is not guaranteed until both the contract and retainer are received.

The remaining balance is typically due 7–14 days before the wedding date, depending on the services booked and whether the event is local or a destination wedding.

faq_items.question: Is there a minimum number of services required to book?
faq_items.answer: For most peak-season Saturdays, we require either a bride-only package or a minimum of three paid services (for example, one bride and two bridesmaids). Destination and holiday weekend bookings may have higher minimums. Elopement and City Hall packages may be exempt from these minimums on select dates.

faq_items.question: What bridal hair services do you offer?
faq_items.answer:
We offer a full range of bridal hairstyling services, including:

• Bride-only packages (in-salon, on-site, and select destination locations)
• Bride + party packages for bridesmaids, mothers, and guests
• Individual party member styling (bridesmaids, mothers, flower girls, and guests)
• Bridal hair trials and preview appointments
• Destination wedding hairstyling for select locations such as Maui and wine country

Makeup services may be added through our partner artists upon request.

faq_items.question: Do you style short hair or pixie cuts for weddings?
faq_items.answer: Yes. We style all hair lengths, including short hair and pixie cuts. For very short styles, we typically focus on blowouts, texture, volume, and accessory placement rather than traditional updos. You can select a guest or mother-of-the-bride service for short hair, and well tailor the look to your cut and desired aesthetic.

faq_items.question: What payment methods do you accept?
faq_items.answer:
We accept major credit and debit cards, ACH/bank transfers for larger bookings, and our own Digital Bridal Gift Cards as partial or full payment. Cash may be accepted in-studio for trials and in-salon services.

We do not accept personal checks on the wedding day. All wedding balances must be paid in full before the event date as outlined in your contract.

faq_items.question: Do you offer payment plans?
faq_items.answer: For bookings over a certain amount, we can split your remaining balance (after the retainer) into two or three scheduled payments, as long as the full amount is paid by the due date outlined in your contract. Payment plans must be arranged at the time of booking and require a valid card on file for automatic charges.

faq_items.question: When should I book my bridal hair trial?
faq_items.answer:
We recommend scheduling your bridal hair trial 2–4 months before your wedding date. This timing allows you to have your final dress, veil, and accessories chosen, while still leaving room to make adjustments based on what you learn during the trial.

Weekday afternoon or evening appointments typically have the most availability and allow your stylist extra time to experiment.

faq_items.question: Is a bridal hair trial required?
faq_items.answer:
A bridal hair trial is highly recommended but not strictly required. For complex styles, elaborate accessories, or destination weddings, we strongly suggest a trial so we can test hold, timing, and how your hair behaves in different conditions.

Some packages may include a discounted trial or require a trial for certain high-detail looksthese details will be noted in the package description.

faq_items.question: Where are you located?
faq_items.answer:
Our primary studio is located in San Francisco, near the 94103 zip code area, with partner stylists based throughout the Bay Area. We travel on-site to venues in San Francisco, the Peninsula, the East Bay, wine country, and select destination locations.

Specific travel fees and minimums may apply depending on your getting-ready location.

faq_items.question: How far in advance should I book my wedding hairstyling?
faq_items.answer:
For peak wedding season (April–October), we recommend booking 9–12 months in advance for Saturdays and popular holiday weekends. Off-season and weekday dates may be available closer to your wedding.

If your date is within the next 3 months, please contact us as soon as possiblewe may still be able to accommodate you, especially for in-salon or elopement packages.
